---
title: Sad Songs & Pretty Charts - a Gosta Berling Music Data Visualization
author: Greg Dubrow
date: '2021-02-26'
slug: sad-songs-pretty-charts-a-gosta-berling-music-data-visualization
draft: true
categories:
  - post
tags:
  - rstats
  - R
  - dataviz
  - data visualization
  - ggplot
  - music
  - gosta berling
  - spotify
subtitle: ''
summary: ''
authors: []
lastmod: 'r format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")'
featured: no
image:
  caption: ''
  focal_point: ''
  preview_only: no
projects: []
---

## Post still in draft stage...even though I have a draft: true in the YAML slug it's been published (thanks to my inadvertant push to git) & rather than tempt the blogdown/Hugo fates by deleting the file, it's up. Ignore for now, full post to come.

## Using the Spoitfy API and spotifyr package to visualize some music I've made

For this post, I thought I'd focus on music analytics, given that music and data science/analysis are two things I've probably spent most of my waking hours on for a number of years now. 

Over the years I've made a lot of music in a number of different projects. For most of my time living in the Bay Area I've played with some friends in a band called [Gosta Berling](https://gostaberling.bandcamp.com/). We've released two EPs and a full album *(click on the album covers to give listen)*

[![Winterland](images/gb_winterland_sm.jpg)](https://gostaberling.bandcamp.com/album/winterland) [![Travel](images/gb_travel_sm.jpg)](https://gostaberling.bandcamp.com/album/travel) [![Everybody's Sweetheart](images/gb_sweetheart_sm.jpg)](https://gostaberling.bandcamp.com/album/everybodys-sweetheart)

Our sound could be called melancholy mood-pop. We like melody, but we were raised on post-punk so that minor key vibe is definitely present. The Spotify API has musical features including danceability, energy, and valence (what they call 'happiness'), and I thought I'd try and learn Charlie Thompson's [`spotifyr`](https://github.com/charlie86/spotifyr) package and see how we score. `spotifyr` has a bunch of functions designed to make it easier to navigate Spotify's JSON data structure. 

So first thing, let's load the packages we'll be using:
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "images/",
  out.width = "100%") 
```

```{r pkg load, message=FALSE, ECHO = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidylog)
library(httr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggrepel)
library(spotifyr)
library(janitor)
library(GGally)
library(PerformanceAnalytics)
library(corrr)

```
